title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation · Object-Lifetime Lens: Reclamation Strategy
summary: A lock-free metrics cache removes nodes while readers still hold addresses and ordinary tests rarely reproduce the race. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem reclamation strategy increment in threaded lifetime and reclamation proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Reclamation Strategy · Theory Reclamation Strategy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2519. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
